feed.title: Lizstar’s Trashcan
feed.subtitle: A place where Lizstar writes about the stupid junk she wants to write about. Special interests include yuri, video games, writing, yuri, obscure video games, and a lot of yuri.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Picard Season 1 episodes 8 - 10
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I was not looking forward to DIS, but I think the thing I’ve been dreading the most was this. I have NOT been looking forward to this. Every thing about this sounds bad.

Broken pieces - PIC 2/10

God, TELL ME ABOUT IT, title.

The last time on has a lot of stuff about Rios. My guess is he’ll die this episode, or next episode.

We start with the Zhat Vash or whatever they’re called, which are a secret order INSIDE the secret order. They’re in a like, cult meeting where they’re standing around a glowing thing on THE GRIEF PLANET, as it’s called. They get visions from some artifact thing on it about how all of life is going to be killed by AI. THEY GOT A FUCKING REAPER VISION. And then as the new generation of Zhat Vash sees the vision, all of them kill themselves except for the incest girl, who’s like “WE MUST STOP THEM.”

We also learn the Romulan fortune teller who was borged and insane, adopted the incest twins, and that’s why she knew about the future, she saw the vision from THE GRIEF PLANET. Also her grief about this is why the borg cube broke. Everything about this is stupid. Also it’s trying REALLY hard to make Incest Girl sympathetic now? You can’t do that THE EPISODE AFTER THE LAST ONE. Did you all forget her torturing Hugh, killing innocent ex borg just for the fuck of it, and then killing him just cause?

Back on the Millenium Falcon, things are tense. They do at LEAST realize that the doctor is a spy. But this also means Rios and Raffi don’t trust Soji, at all, and think this is going to fall apart. Rios is also super checked out, and wants out as soon as he transports them to Deep Space 12. He ALSO calls for Starfleet to send a fleet out to help him, and they do. Okay then? NOW they want to do that??

We get a scene where Seven debates reintigrating the ex borg so she can take the borg cube out. But if she does that, might not want to be released after, and she might not want to release them. Then, immediately after, we get a scene with Raffi talking to all 4 of Rios’ clone holograms discussing why he’s so upset. THIS. SHOW. HAS. A. TONE. PROBLEM.

You know, when they said “We’re making a sequel to TNG that brings back elements from the show”, I wasn’t expecting them to do the clashing A, B, and C plots. This scene is really hard to follow btw. No shade on this actor but there are 4 of them, their only differences is they have different accents, and he is NOT Brent Spiner.

Doctor’s awake. She doesn’t want to talk about the vision she saw. She’s like “do you believe in hell? I didn’t. Now I do.” Okay.

We need to talk about Roko’s Basilisk.

This FEELS kinda like a take on Roko’s Basilisk? And if you dunno what that is, content warning, because some fucking idiots view it as a cognito hazard. Basically, it’s a super AI in the future that will become a god. If you don’t help it to exist, it will torment you to hell. It is Pascal’s Wager, just straight up, but for tech bro AI dipshits. Some people have actually killed themselves over this. This reminds me of that. But it doesn’t have a good thing to say on the topic, or much anything.

We get a scebe wgere Rios finally explains his dark traumatic past, but it’s interrupted with edits where it cuts back and forth to the Doctor harassing and interrogating the AI, like “DO YOU EVEN DREAM? DO YOU CRY? DO YOU EAT?” Fuck off, lady, I’m trying to understand the sexy man’s trauma to see if he’s fixable.

So apparently some point in the past, his captain got some envoys from someplace, then a message from Starfleet. A “black flag order”, which means follow this order or your entire ship will be destroyed. That’s not a thing Starfleet has. You cannot convince me of that. Anyways, the captain then killed the envoys violently, then himself. Rios saw all of this and got severe PTSD about it. Honestly, understandable. Will anything happen to the starfleet higherups who ordered this? :) Probably nooooot! Also one of the emmissaries looks just like Soji, so she’s like, a recreation of her.

Seven sees Incest Lady is STILL killing exborg for LITERALLY no reason, and decides fuck it, bring them into the fold to try and save them. It doesn’t work, they’re all killed anyway, thrown out into space. Dude, what IS it with modern Trek and its love of spacing people?

From learning about this tragic backstory, Raffi pieces EVERYTHING together. She’s like “Oh, Commodore Oh is half Romulan.” How did you know that? “Also the Romulans did 9/11” I mean, sure. This is obvious shit I discovered as soon as the characters showed up. Romulans want to kill all AI because they assume from a random thing they found on a rock that AI will kill everyone. Soji hijacks the ship and flies to a Borg warp center, which is apparently only 9 hours away?

Incest Lady gets away so Seven re-borging herself means nothing. But it doesn’t matter cause she breaks away anyway.

Rios is like “Oh okay, since Oh gave the order, it’s fine cause that means Starfleet didn’t betray us and isn’t evil” and Picard goes “No, no they still are, because THEY Were the ones who decided to do an evil ban on AIs.” Oh good, glad we aren’t absolving starfleet of their sins. But also I still hate Starfleet being weirdly unrepentantly evil in modern Trek.

This entire episode was weirdly hard to follow, honestly.

Et in Arcadia Ego Part 1 - PIC 2/10

They make it to the homeworld. Alongside the Borg Cube, and also Incest Brother who was somehow still tailing them, THROUGH A TRANSWARP BEACON. They are all subsequently eaten by a giant orchid-like space plants that drag them down to the planet. Including the borg cube. No. No, that’s dumb. Soji: “They used an EMP” THAT’S ALSO VERY DUMB. IF BORGS WERE RESISTANT TO EMPS WOLF 359 WOULDN’T HAVE HAPPENED.

They crash down, the ship out of energy. Picard wakes up and decides to tell everyone the plan. He will take Soji to her people, and tell them “I am dying btw, it’s terminal. Don’t treat me like I am, you’ll piss me off”. “Remember that scene in the sequel trilogy in Star Trek where C3PO thinks he’s going to die and says “I’m just taking one last look at all my good friends” and looks at the people he’s known for 2 hours? This reminds me of that.” - Neli

The Borg cube landed nearby so they head to it. Yeah uh, no. Those explode so no one can get the technology. They do that. I guess MAYBE the Borg could have turned that off? But okay.

If there’s anything I can say, at least Seven is hot. They lament Hugh for a single sentence then move on.

The Zhat Vash, notably a secret organization of a secret organization that no one thinks exist, are sending 218 romulan warbirds. Reminder that Wolf 359 was 40 ships.

“It’s all on you now” Picard says, leaving Elnor (I legit typed elrond by accident) and Seven behind as he walks into the light. God, I’d be such a good director if this is all it takes.

We reach AI Heaven. We also meet SOONG! Not that Soong, his son actually. This is the THIRD Soong Brent Spiner has played. If you think for even 2 minutes, Soong having a secret son we never heard about before is kinda weird, but look. I’m happy to see Brent Spiner again.

ONE OF THESE AI HAVE LEARNED HOW TO MIND MELD. THAT REQUIRES PSYCHIC ABILITY, AND SHE IS A ROBOT. WHAT? Well at least this one is CONSENSUAL I GUESS.

Fuck this noise so hard.

They think that the message the Vhat Zash found isn’t actually meant for organic minds, but instead for artificial minds. It’ll just drive organics insane. She…. mind melds, and finds out what the message is SUPPOSED to be. We get a weird message with video behind it, INCLUDING A FUCKING FOX DYING AND DECOMPOSING, REALLY WANTED TO SEE THAT, THANK YOU STAR TREK. Basically, at some point in the past, an AI species killed its creators after they turned on them and tried to destroy them. They then went to another dimension, but left a message behind of “hey, synthetics, call for us and we’ll help you kill all organic life.”

The Vulcan synth is now REALLY evil, going to others and going “we should kill organics actually :)” Oh great. Also they drag Incest Boy in.

This episode is giving lots of death flags for Picard. But it’s ALSO showing that Soong is developing a kind of mind transfer device, SO IT’S REALLY OBVIOUS WHAT’S GOING TO HAPPEN.

Narek, Incest Boy, tries to emotionally manipulate Soji again. She tells him to fuck off and he goes “Well it’s fine cause we’ll just kill you all anyway :)” he then manipulates another innocent synth on the planet. Can this guy die yet? Nope, the Evil Cyborg lets him out, he kills the innocent one, and runs off, now working with her. She uses this death to prove that the organics should all be killed. Soong is all good for this for some reason. I guess cause he’s coded to be dying and will mind transfer himself?? Wait IT’S JUST ROKO’S BASILISK AGAIN ISN’T IT. GOD DAMMIT.

Hey, remember how all of these AI arte based off of Data? Remember how Data was unstable and wanted to kill all humans? No? You DON’T remember that??? They end up arresting Picard and decide to kill all Humans. Oh goodie.

One more. We’re almost done.

Et in Arcadia Ego Part 1 - PIC 1.5/10

We are sitting here waiting for the final battle to come as it clearly is. Picard is like “please don’t let the Romulans turn you into the monsters they fear”. Look, if I was more engaged in this, maybe I’d be like “wow, that’s a terrible thing to say to people trying to keep alive who are hunted and hated by the entire galaxy”.

Wait, I just… realized something, watching the incest twins talk about the prophecy. So, they follow the prophecy. But it’s not real, it’s nonsense put into their brains from a beacon meant for synthetics saying “call us and we’ll kill all Humans :)”. But… they knew things that only a prophecy could explain. They knew the destroyer, and the messenger, they knew they were twin robots, and could tell who was which. Okay.

The doctor joins Soong and the synths in killing all Humans, because she’s “their mother”. But she’s clearly going to just kill them to try and stop this.

This dude calls Romulan Fuckboy a “snakehead”, which is 100% a slur. He ends up teaming up with them to stop the AI. Uh, dude, YOU’RE THE ONE WHO DID THIS AND STARTED THIS. Not gonna own up to that? No? Okay. We get a scene of them doing a heist, sneaking fuckboi back in. They do that classic heist scene, them talking about the setup, interspersed with them doing it.

Soong FINALLY looks through the murdered AI’s data and finds out, yeah, clearly, it was the evil android. So he joins them for their heist. Okay, sure, he’s on our side again and is good. What the FUCK is going on with everyone’s sides here? The doctor also breaks Picard out.

Picard: “They have to learn what living is for. And I will lead by example.” He steals Rios’ ship and goes off to fight the Romulans. I’m sure Rios will LOVE that. Soong also shuts down the evil AI? But uh… doesn’t actually do anything? To stop the other AI from building the Kill Humans Device???

The Romulans show up. More of those orchid plant ships come up to meet them. Dude, 5 of those orchids killed a borg cube, how the fuck couldn’t they kill this fleet? There’s ten of them.

Seven kills incest sister. Thank god. They were fighting btw.

“Soji, I will give you something I hope will make you reconsider killing all Humans. My life.” The doctor, next to him, thinking “WAIT, I DIDN’T AGREE TO THIS”.

Picard does the Picard maneuver. Using the eyeball thingy they stole from the dead AI, they make a million fake images. This holds them off just long enough fooooor

A hundred copies of the exact same 3D ship file they had to show up. Kind of embarassing.

At least he’s here. God bless you, Frakes. He’s like “Alright Commodore or General or whatever the hell you’re talking about. I’ll say stand down, but I’d love nothing more than to kick your Tal Shiar ass.” I love him. Actually good line.

THE EVIL AI FROM ANOTHER GALAXY ARE WRITHING MECHANICAL SPACE WORMS. WHY ARE THEY SPACE WORMS?? Anyways, Soji breaks the beacon, General Oh fucks off, and Riker is like “What was I supposed to do? Just sit there and eat pizza?” and then leaves. No, please, PLEASE stay. Picard immediately has a Brain Attack and dies.

There’s no tension or stakes here. Because they ALREADY SHOWED HOW THEY’RE GONNA BRING HIM BACK FROM THE DEAD. Also there are two more seasons after this, of course. We get signs of them all sad and crying around Picard’s dead body, but like, c’mon.

We then get a bunch of scenes of everyone sitting around sad and crying in pairs. Wow, they’re going HARD on this aren’t they? A butterfly flies between each of the scenes, cause butterflies are human souls or some shit. Then boom, he’s alive again. Kind of.

They are in a simulation. Data has a like, pearly gates talk with Picard. And gives him closer on his death. And then is like “you have to go, goodbye Captain”, as he walks into the light. But he asks for one last thing. “Please kill me”. His consciousness is still here, through that neuron Mattox harvested, but he wants to die to finally be Human-like.

HEY.

DO YOU KNOW WHAT I NEEDED TO SEE?

DATA DYING AGAIN.

I AM GOING TO MORB. I’M GOING TO BECOME A BIBLICALLY ACCURATE MORB.

Watching my favorite Trek character die, SHOULD have been like… some kind of emotion. Mostly it’s just annoyance.

Anyways yes, Picard is obviously revived in this golem. He’s now a replicant. No super powers, no AI anything. He’ll still naturally die in 20 years, just have his body turn off. Which is even more fucked up tbh. But gotta have that… status quo I guess.

We get a scene where Picard talks about how great Data was, in the show called Picard, as they turn his brain off and kill him, again.

Why are we suffer?

Just to suffer?

They all get in the ship, the AI ban is lifted off screen, and Picard says “engage”, and it ends.

I hated this. The entirety of this season. It is, in fact, worse than Discovery season 1. At least that didn’t assassinate characters with bad writing. It just had bad writing.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 226 - Minesweeper
entry.content:
For the past week or so I’ve contemplated if I should skip this one. Would it be weird to have a catalogue of every single game I’ve done on Retro Achievements, but go from 225 to 227? Would that invite more dialogue and mystery than the ACTUAL game #226, Minesweeper, could hold?

It’s not like this is for anyone. It’s basically just an excuse for me to write about games I play. Fun little dialogues and reviews. But what the hell do you say about Minesweeper? Genuinely and honestly?

Well… this is an MSX port of Minesweeper. It’s from 2023/2024, and was made by Under4Mhz. You can find it on itch. It is… a port of Minesweeper. To the MSX.

There are three settings. Small, medium, and large. It’s a logic puzzle. It- who am I kidding, you know what Minesweeper is. I was never the LARGEST Minesweeper fan, my Windows time waster of choice was actually Hearts, but I’ve played some games that take the formula and twist it wildly on their head. Puzzle games that really recontextualize what minesweeper really is. Going back to basics, however, Minesweeper is… fine, and good. But it’s a time waster. And boy did this waste my time.

It took me two hours to beat Minesweeper three times. That sounds bad, and it is. I have no idea if the regular ol’ Windows XP Minesweeper is this bad, but this kept giving me unwinnable states. It’s VERY easy to make a board where you get everything but one corner, and then it’s just a fucking 50/50 on whether or not the last square is a mine. I never did too well as a kid to get that far on the large boards, so I have no idea how common that was, but it happened to me THREE TIMES HERE. I failed the coin flip twice.

Look, do you want to play Minesweeper? Play any of the handful of modern iterations. You don’t need to bust out an MSX.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 227 - Snake & Rhino In The Sketchbook

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 227 - Snake & Rhino In The Sketchbook

entry.content:
Now THIS is what I’m talking about!! Snake & Rhino is a homebrew MSX game by ARC8, and it has style for daaaaays. Unbelievably pretty for an MSX game. It’s a simple puzzle game. You’ve got Snake, and you’ve got Rhino. You can control either of them at a time, and you need to get them both to the exit. Sometimes you press switches to open doors. Sometimes you just go to the exit. Simple as that.

They both move a little differently and have a few rules to them. Snake will slide in one direction until something stops him. Rhino can walk to anywhere he wants to go. Snake can climb up and down ladders. Rhino can not. Snake can go over cracks in the floor. Rhino will fall.

All of the letters are cool looking Escher designs. Seriously, this game just makes my neurons light up to look at it. It’s probably the best looking MSX game I’ve seen. I also just really like MC Escher paintings. Y’all ever play Echochrome? Well, anyway.

Snake and Rhino isn’t a very long game. It has around 30 worlds, and it took me just over an hour to complete all of them. There’s a few brain ticklers in there, but for the most part you’ll be able to get through with no problem, while wanting just a bit more.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Picard Season 1 episodes 5 - 7
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I was not looking forward to DIS, but I think the thing I’ve been dreading the most was this. I have NOT been looking forward to this. Every thing about this sounds bad. Only 3 episodes tonight so we can pace this a little bit.

Stardust City Rag - PIC 2/10

Hey, remember Icheb? I liked Icheb! He was a cool ex borg from Voyager, basically Seven’s surrogate child. Anyways this episode starts with him being brutally tortured and killed to harvest borg tech from him. What in the shit fuck? Seven then kills him to put him out of his misery.

THIS SHOW. IS SO FUCKED UP. We REALLY did not need to see this, they didn’t have to kill off a beloved character for this. They could have just gone “Oh, Seven didn’t go into starfleet and is here to help cause she wants to”, make up a reason that doesn’t fucking depress me.

This guy straight up looks like the Goombas from the Maria movies. Anyways, Maddox is poisoned and killed somewhere in some fancy bar by some fancy lady, but not before admitting he’s been working with the Tal Shiar.

Seven is a “Fenris Ranger” now. They’re basically old school lawmen in the lawless corners of space, trying to keep peace where they can. It’s not glamarous, but she’s doing her best. But she’s kinda sad about it, her life’s not going hot. Gee, I SURE DO LOVE HOW EVERY CHARACTER’S LIFE IS MISERABLE NOW THAT THEIR SHOWS ENDED.

We’re randomly interrupted by cartoon character holograms advertisements being goofy and weird, like pop up ads. They give me Bonzi Boddy energy. This show has a tone problem.

They decide they need to get to some tal shiar lady. And to do that, they use Seven as bait, since she loves to buy Ex Borg slaves on the black market. They go down to Shitty Space Vegas, where apparently Mott the barber from TNG has opened a barber empire? We also mention Quark. These references feel… unearned. I like how this show feels allergic to like, bringing in TNG stuff and giving it justice and making it happy but they sure do love to REFERENCE it. Anyways, Rios goes to meet with this guy who can smell really well to cinch a deal. They give him drugs so he’s blazed out of his mind as he does it. This seems like… a very BAD plan.

Picard is going to pretend to be a pirate. This is totally why Picard signed on to this series, but I love the idea that all he has to do is put on a ratty outfit and an eyepatch, and he can pretend to be a slave trading pirate. Dude’s one of the most famous people in space. It’d be like Barrack Obama trying to pretend to be a pirate.

Raffi goes to see her son, who is NOT happy to see her, and wants her out. Apparently he has a Romulan wife and they’re expecting a kid. Good for them. Let’s make the characters even MORE miserable I guess. Anyways ha ha more Picard funny pirate voice! THIS SERIES HAS A TONE PROBLEM!

We find out that Seven was just using Picard to get to this woman. I mean yeah, obviously, but they ALSO find Maddox with her, so I guess she did at least help. As she explains how this woman betrayed her, we have to watch Icheb die and be tortured again. YEs, we saw that, we’re good, show. She seems to decide not to do revenge to protect the others, beaming up, then basically beams RIGHT back down and fucking pastes this girl.

What was the point of… any of this?

Meanwhile Maddox explains what he knows to Picard, and then when Picard goes off, Aggie, the doctor with them, basically smothers him to death. She turns off his machines and lets him die, in a REALLY uncomfortably gross scene, while she sobs and goes “I wish you knew what I knew”. Gee it sure is going to be a surprise how he died, ESPECIALLY SINCE THE EMH SAW HER DO IT.

This episode was all over the place. This SERIES is all over the place.

The Impossible Box - PIC 2.5/10

Soji, the android, has a nightmare about Maddox dying. Then she wakes up with Incestuous Fuckboi Romulan I can’t remember the name of.

Wow, gee, I can’t believe Maddox died all by himself with no foul play. Gee, what a surprise. I can’t believe it. Out of all these character btw, the one I most tolerate is Elnor. While he interjects in scenes and does nothing else, what he says is kinda amusing, and I like him. He’s almost got pseudo Data energy. Everyone else I could take or leave, tbh.

The doctor and the Han Solo clone randomly start making out. What the fuck is going on.

Oh, THIS is why we have the shitty holographic screens. So we could do this one shot.

We get more scenes of incestuous fuck boi and fuck girl. I want you to know, I’m not just reading too much into it, I don’t WANT to invent incest, she’s fondling his god damn face for some reason. Anyways they’re doing that thing again. That thing where they want to say a profound sentence so they invent a scene of shitty sentences around it. This time it’s about a Romulan puzzle box. Cool.

Raffi burns the bridge of her last friend to help Picard, AGAIN, and then wanders off crying and drinking. This gets a heroic fanfare for some reason. Forget a tone problem, this show is tone drinking. Also it’s just now hitting me, but Raffi’s character is… she’s a black absentee parent with drug problems. And her current problems are because of a white guy using her and then throwing her away. Whoof.

Soji has a breakdown where she thinks something’s wrong with her, and she carbon dates herself and her stuff and finds out she’s only 37 months old.

Picard has another panic attack as he enters the Borg Cube, freaking out about the Locutus thing. Hugh shows up and helps snap him out of it. We also FINALLY find out what the Artifact, as they call it (the borg cube) is even here for. They’re not like, harvesting it for tech and parts. They’re actually rehabilitating Borg and unassimilating them, the ones that were on ice down below. Picard is stunned that the Romulans are doing this out of generosity. Dude, there are a lot of heroic Romulans in this show.

The Romulan Fuckboi mind melds with Soji basically, using some weird Romulan meditation room. He uses this to find out where she’s from. They find out she was built on a planet with two moons that appear red in a thunder storm. Yeah, that’ll be easy to find in the entire fucking galaxy, dumbass. And then he puts some gas emmitting thing in the room with her so it’ll kill her. Radiation, apparently. You know, cause Data was famously affected by Radiation. She punches her way through the room, as Hugh and Picard rush to try and find her. They find her first, then use a magical teleporter to escape. For some reason, Elnor stays behind to keep them safe. Why? Dude, why?

This episode literally fridge logic’d me. It’s the morning after now, I was making breakfast, and as my water was boiling I was like “wait a second”. Why the hell does the Borg Queen have a specialized long-range teleporter? They really skip past that part. Apparently it’s in the queen’s room, and needs an XB to activate it. And then you input a planet and… off you go. Yeah, uh, why does the Borg Queen have an Iconian Gateway??? They had far reaching tech, but uh, it was based in GIANT structures and pre-built pathways. This is not that. I feel if they had this, they could have just… plopped a few drones on Earth and gone to town with a zombie apocalypse.

This show really REALLY does things without thinking about them. Just seriously, it’s one of two categories, every time: Oh, this seems cool, or oh, I didn’t think that far ahead. In this case it’s both. Woah, big teleporter cool, AND “how the fuck is Picard gonna get off this Borg cube”. JUST WRITE A BETTER SHOW, DUDE.

Nepenthe - PIC 3.5/10

We start with a scene explaining why the Doctor is there, and why she killed Maddox. The Vulcan secret intelligence leader, who we KNOW is a Romulan, mind melded with her and showed her something bad will happen if synthetics can exist. She also injests a tracker for some reason. Okay. Meanwhile, back in the present, Hugh and Elnor did not do a good job of holding them off, and are now captured. Hugh watches as the incest lady kills all of the borg on the cube, cause apparently ALL of this was set up to find this synth, even though they already had her on the cube the entire time and knew she was there, and they don’t actually care about helping the ex borg??? Elnor is not here, but then suddenly after they leave and leave Hugh crying alone, Elnor lopes out of the shadows with his fucking sword. I guess he was just hiding???

Meanwhile, Picard and Soji land on a planet and are immediately accosted by some girl with a bow and arrow. Apparently this is Kestral. That name MIGHT be familiar to you. Picard asks to see her mom and dad, and she takes them to see them.

IT’S RIKER AND TROI! They live in a nice little log cabin in the woods with their kids, apparently. Troi looks amazing. Riker looks aged as hell but he sounds amazing and as SOON as he saw Picard he was like “Aww MAN!” and hugged him bigly. Holy shit, you have NO idea how emotional I’m getting seeing Riker just be Riker. I’m sure he’s suffered through some horrible bullshit the past several years that made him sad and awful, but currently they just seem retired and have to deal with “Kzinti raids”. HOLY SHIT, KZINTI MENTIONED. Their daughter, Kestral, is awesome as hell btw. She’s named after Troi’s sister, Kestral, from that one episode. She brings in a “bunnicorn” she hunted, and Troi’s like “You DID remove its venom sacks, right?” “No mom, I was hoping we could all spew black bile and die”. She’s great.

Okay turns out they ARE sad, their first child died. He would have been 18. They’re doing okay, but yeah, they ALSO are suffering from Tragedy Background-itis. Apparently his name was Thaddius, and he was super creative. He invented a bunch of fake languages, which they still speak around the house. Man that sounds awesome, why’d they invent a cool character then immediately kill him off in the background??

Soji is VERY suddenly acting like an Android. She does a head tilt which Riker recognizes at Data’s head tilt. “I recognize that head tilt anywhere!” Oh, the head tilt she never did ever before???

Hugh is EXTREMELY suddenly killed off by the incestuous Romulan. He then has a moment with Elnor as he dies. Glad we had this moment with Hugh instead of any of the characters he has a connection to. WHERE IS GEORDI???

With the help of Riker and Troi’s mom and dad vibes, they get Soji to trust them more, and she tells them about her homeworld, and asks them to take her there. Picard promises to, and then they find where it is.

The doctor, feeling remorse for leading them all into a trap, injects herself with some kind of poison. This for SOME reason shuts down the tracking device in her body??? What?! And then we also see Elnor summoning Seven of Nine with a distress beacon. Lots of shit happening with these side characters. Can I see more Riker again?

Thank you. Riker and Picard walk through the woods and drink some tea. Nice. They see Picard and Soji off, and we get another misplaced TNG motif.

This was better just because Jonathan Frakes is a nearly perfect Human being.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Picard Season 1 episodes 1 - 4
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I was not looking forward to DIS, but I think the thing I’ve been dreading the most was this. I have NOT been looking forward to this. Every thing about this sounds bad.

But its time to get started

Rememberence - PIC 3/10

We get Picard and Data on the Enterprise D, playing Poker. They’re having a pleasant time. Data plays a 5 of a kind of queens, and then Picard sees they’re above Mars, and then the ship explodes.

Also, to make Data not look old here, they lathered SO much shit on him. It looks… a little uncanny valley. Anyways, Picard wakes up from his nightmare. He’s on a chataeu in France, with his pitbull pupper named Number One. Everything’s fine.

Anyways, random twist to some random woman on a date in her apartment. Suddenly people break into her room, kill her boyfriend, and try to kidnap her. She then beats the shit out of them easily, before having a weird mind blast of Picard.

Is that a fucking Halo gun

Anyways back to Picard. He’s met with some lady and guy, the guy’s name is Xhaban, the girl is Laris, they’re clearly Romulans, and they need him to do a thing for them. He’s kinda grumpy he has to. Turns out it’s an interview about the Romulan supernova.

Hey, remember that? The Romulan supernova? That happened. It was a thing in the movies they could have EASILY said was an alternate universe, but decided it HAD to be a thing. Alright, sure. Anyways, for this interview, they promised to not talk to him about the one thing he doesn’t want to talk about, but they do anyway: the terrorist attack on Mars.

Some synthetic life forms did a terrorist attack killing thousands on Mars, and Synths were banned as a result in the Federation. Picard still thinks it was a mistake. He’s annoyed at this obvious attempt of a spin, gives one last announcement of “Starfleet fucked up, they made a bad decision, they left people behind, and I would have nothing to do with that. Goodbye,” as the lady from the weird murder scene watches the TV. This is a pretty clunky way to exposit what happened.

She shows up at Picard’s and tells him what happened. He has no idea who the hell she is, and she doesn’t know why she’s here, but the same thing that lead her to fight and kill her would be kidnappers leads her here. We find out her name is Dahj. The dialogue about this is kinda… clunky, and slow. The pacing is kinda off.

Picard has another Data Dream, then wakes up to find Dahj is gone. Picard gets antsy, seems to realize something, and rushes off to his archive. He has the Captain Picard Day banner, that’s cute.

He finds what he’s looking for. A painting made by Data, years ago, which has Dahj in it, named “Daughter”. Dahj finds him again, after having a weird conversation with some creepy fake-mom, and Picard tells her about the painting. Once again, the writing and pacing is just… off, I can’t really describe it.

He tells her he thinks she’s an android, and her memories are fake. She doesn’t take it well.

VERY suddenly, some of those masked guys show up again and fight. There’s a giant fight scene, then one of them spits some acid on her. Her skin melts off, she screams, and explodes, throwing Picard away.

What the fuck??

Why even set that up to just do this?!

Picard wakes up back at his home. All the security footage shows is just him. He’s not happy, and says he’s been waiting for die. Then we get a scene where he’s talking to a scientist about possibly making a flesh and blood android. She laughs at it and cracks jokes. This is not a scene that should show up right after the previous scenes.

We find out some history. They have B-4 still here, he’s deactivated. We learn Data TRIED to download his neural net into B-4 but almost all of it was lost, thus retconning what was planned after Nemesis, and what all the books wrote, and what Star Trek Online did. We learn that Maddox disappeared a while ago. Remember him? He’s the fucking dickstick from Measure of a Man. Also we learn that if androids ARE made, they are made in pairs. Hey it’s okay, we have a backup. Oh boy.

We get a scene of Dahj’s sister, Soji, talking to some guy named Narek. He’s obviously a Romulan pretending he’s not. Then it zooms out, and we’re in a Borg Cube. This is dumb. Alright.

Clunky, and a bad way to start a series.

Rememberence - PIC 2/10

This picture makes me think of Picard in his tighty whities standing with a gun on a roadside.

We get some scenes of 9/11. I’m sorry, I mean the Mars Attacks. We’re introduced to some miners who have a synth working with them as labor. Hey, synthetic mining slaves, why not???? He reminds me of the Crash Test Dummies from the late 80s. Anyways, he suddenly goes rogue, violently kills everyone, then shoots himself in the head after hacking some defend stuff. This happens all over the planet.

I love how happy and optimistic new trek is.

Anyways back on the vineyard, we learn about the Zhat Vash, which is basically an extre secretive cabal of Tal Shiar operatives. A black ops INSIDE the Romulan black ops. They also infiltrate everywhere, “even the Gorn Hegemony”. Dude, I wanna watch THAT show, with a Romulan agent trying to infiltrate the Gorn. This ex-Tal Shiar who’s helping out Picard helps him find out about them. She’s VERY competent and cool. I can’t wait for her to be a major character for this series!! It’s so cool to have a heroic Romulan, especially Tal Shiar.

“This isn’t your daddy’s Star Trek. This is DARK Trek that FUCKS” - Pauline in my Discord

The Romulan who looks like SNW Spock to me and will never NOT look like Spock to me is fucking the android. Okay, cool.

Picard gets a doctor in who tells him he’s got a disease and will probably die soon. One of the side effects of it is “irritability that causes you to lash out inappopriately during interviews”. DUDE. THAT WAS THE MOST INNAPROPRIATE INTERVIEW I’VE EVER SEEN. They promised, 3 times, not to talk about a thing, and then DID talk about it while being overwhelmingly hostile.

There’s something this fucking show is doing a lot. They have like, two “hard hitting” lines the actors REALLY zone in for. But every line around them sucks ass and is poorly written, so they never land! It’s like they’re writing this backwards?

Picard goes to Starfleet like “Hey, Maddox appears to be making new Datas, using data from him. That might mean he can be revived. Please give me a ship so I can go look into this” and Starfleet gets OFFENDED. Like how DARE you, especially after that SHAMEFUL DISPLAY ON THE INTERVIEW. God, we’re gonna get this a lot aren’t we? She’s angry cause Picard wanted to save the Romulans and wasn’t around to stop the Mars attacks, as if he knew they were coming, and said he should have just let them die, cause they were our enemies, who cares. Man, this poor actress. Imagine being told you’re gonna act in Star Trek alongside SIR PATRICK STEWART and they do this to you.

We get some more scenes of the other android and her Romulan boytoy. They are on the borg cube doing research, I think? It’s really not explained what the fuck they’re doing. She’s kinda just standing there watching someone do surgery on a dead Borg.

You know what we don’t have enough of? Scenes of Picard being restless. Oh, and outside shots of his chataeu for like 10 seconds. He gets a combadge and goes “I need your help, I need a ship”.

We get that lady from earlier talking to a Commodore, telling her about Picard saying Romulan operatives are active here. This Vulcan, who is totally not a Romulan operative in disguise, goes “if that was true, I would know”. She then calls for a Lieutenant Rizzo. Holy shit I would LOVE Star Trek x Muppets. Yeah, obviously she’s a Romulan spy. Like, duh. We find that out instantly at least, they don’t let that sit for a while.

Can I say, I’m disappointed by how they’re handling the Romulans here? They set it up so that we could have Romulans in a different light. Heroic ones. And we do! But we do very little with them and leave them behind, and then still have Romulans here who are like Lannisters, scheming and conniving and murdering.

Picard visits some hot lesbian and gives her wine. She hates him but listens when he mentions Romulans. Alright. The uh, the end of the episode? This series is just bad scene after bad scene with no theme to connect em. THis is not ever what I wanted for a Star Trek.

Rememberence - PIC 2/10

This is such a “woe, plague be upon you” image.

We learn who Raffi is. She used to work under Picard, and after all the shit that went down years ago, Picard didn’t fight Starfleet’s dumb-ass decisions. He accepted a resignition they wanted to force upon him, and she was just straight up fired. Why? What? That’s not… how Starfleet works. And in the present, she’s like “you see I live in a HOVEL”. Girl, we live post scarcity, and you live next to Vasquez Rocks. You CHOOSE to live like this. She also once again calls the interview a disaster. Dude, what the fuck, why are we focusing on that?

We’re back to the Borg Cube, introduced to some ex-borg guy, who talks about the racism he and his people have to face. You know, Star Trek, known for its racism. And then he says he’s gonna give the synthetic a job. I’m glad we get scenes about things we never get explained to us. We STILL dunno what they’re doing on this Borg cube.

Raffi tells Picard to fuck right off, but gives him connections to a pilot.

WHY IS THIS SHOW A BUNCH OF DISCONNECTED SCENES THAT HAVE BARELY ANY IMPACT AND TLEL US NOTHING. WHY AM I EVEN TRYING TO SCRIBE THIS DOWN?!

We’re introduced to Picard’s new pilot, who is very Star Wars, though Picard is like “AH YOU’RE STARFLEET TO THE CORE” even though he is clearly not, at all. Nice informed trait, show. This guy is very very Star Wars Rogue One coded.

There’s another random violent fight scene as Picard chats with his Romulan buddies. We also get the doctor who told us about synths and Maddox back. They capture a Romulan and torture him to get information. Cool!

Oh hey, the ex-Borg guy is finally named. It’s Hugh! Remember him?! Cool! Can’t wait for them to do nothing with him. This show does like to re-introduce characters who were in one good episode. First Maddox, and now Hugh.

Anyways we get a lot of things as the two plotlines begin to co-align. There’s some weird prophecy shit about the synth being “the destroyer”. There’s also a lot of things in this where it’s like, a big climactic scene, then the show just continues. It’s SO weirdly paced.

Wtf is this Sith Hallway? Also these two are siblings and there’s some EXTREME Game of Thrones incest energy coming off of them. “I can smelllll her on you. It smells…. carnalll” WHAT IS GOING ON.

Picard goes on his trip finally, it’s time for adventure. He says Engage, nerds, prepare to cream yourself. It’s him, Star Wars guy, Agnes the AI scientist, and Raffi is here for right now apparently, but she tells them where Maddox is, then they blast off.

Absolute Candor - PIC 4/10

We start 14 years ago, Picard going down to make sure that some Romulans who are relocating to a Federation colony are settling in well. He meets and makes friends with a young Romulan boy named Elnor. Although Picard is not, and has never been, amazing with kids, he’s actually amazing with kids. And while fencing with him, he learns of the 9/11 attacks, promises to be back soon, then leaves. And we of course knows he will not, because he resigns and this entire thing was swept under the rug.

Back in the present, Picard orders an emergency stop on that planet. Raffi is VERY angry about this. Picard also sets up a holodeck of his study in his chataeu cause WE BUILT THIS SET WE BETTER MAKE THE MOST OUT OF IT. Picard wants to go there to get a “Romulan Warrior Nun” to help them out. Their words, not mine.

Picard beams down, past some “ROMULAN ONLY” bars where Romulans glare at him, and seem to call in for something, as he goes to the warrior nuns. They’re at least friendly to see him, and we also meet…

We needed a Legolas on our team. I think.

Romulan-Spock shows the synth I can never remember an “ancient Borg ritual”. It involves taking your shoes off and running through the vent escape. Uh, Borg don’t have shoes. This is such a “ha ha, they’re so in love” scene which is weird since for some reason we already KNOW this guy is Tal Shiar and is evil.

“Picard, because you couldn’t save all of us, you decided to save none of us.” Dog, he lost all of the power he had. He’s just one dude in his 80s. The child he knew and befriended, Elnor, Picard TRIES to get him to go with them, but he refuses, angry that Picard abandoned him so many years ago. I mean, look. THIS kid feeling this way, I get it.

Picard then goes to the segregated bar, rips the “ROMULANS ONLY” sign down and forces his way into the bar. Why is he doing this? He MUST know he’s 80 years old and surrounded by angry racists. I guess he’s trying to bait Legolas into protecting him.

PICARD WITH REAL FENCING ACTION.

Anyways Elrond, sorry Elnor, chops this dude’s fucking head off. DARK TREK THAT FUCKS.

WHY IS THERE SO MUCH SEXUAL TENSION BETWEEN THESE TWO SIBLINGS?!

Some ship attacks Picard’s, and they manage to beat it. It starts exploding, and then they’re like “quick, beam the pilot over!” aaaaand

Seven of Nine. WTF are you doing here??? I mean, it’s nice to see her. She’s one of the ONLY reasons I wanted to watch Picard, cause I knew she was here, but why was she in the Romulan ship?

Anyways this is the best episode so far, but look. This plot in general is just bad. It’s based entirely on one idea: Picard is a galactic pariah because he only saved some people. Both sides hate him. It’d be like if the Jews and the allies fucking HATE Shindler cause he couldn’t save everyone. This just doesn’t make any sense.

I don’t know why we needed Picard to be hated by everyone, just make bad guys who’d hate him cause they oppose him. This really should not be that hard to do, honestly, but they are failing SO hard. And then, the big reason we’d want a TNG followup: TNG characters. We have Hugh, Picard, and probably Maddox, though he hasn’t shown up yet. Two one off characters, and Picard. None of the people we’d ACTUALLY want here. We got a VOYAGER character before we got any of the actual TNG characters. Picard’s reason is “oh I don’t want them risking their lives and commission to help me do the right thing”, you know, LIKE THEY ALWAYS DID ALL THE TIME FOR 30 YEARS.

Just the very concept of this show is… wrong. And it’s poorly written on top of that.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Short Treks 5-8
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

While Disco was being made, they made Short Treks basically at the same time. We should watch those too I guess. This is a Disco side series about the Disco characters. Cool. But this time we ALSO get some Picard stuff in it too, our first introduction to Picard! Oh… boy, I am… SO excited for Picard. Yep. Cannot wait. Yep. Not stalling at all.

Q&A - ST 7.5/10

This is NOT a short trek about Q’s ass, as I expected it might be, instead it’s Spock’s first day on Enterprise. He’s kinda weird here, smiley and young and trying his best to fit in and be the perfect starfleet member and it REALLY feels weird, intentionally.

Una, who I think actually isn’t named, and is just called Number One still, shows him around. It’s a bit awkward. She tries to force conversation by asking Spock to ask her questions. Then they get stuck in the turbolift.

What proceeds is a lot of questions. Spock has two modes. First, he asks really softballs. When Una finds them kinda boring, and asks him to up them, he jumps to deep dark philophical stuff. She asks him to step it back a tad. “Do you like Eggplant?”

It’s kinda funny. As they get MORE stuck in the elevator, it goes to what Spock is like. His aspirations. Why he was smiling. She advices he do his best to “hide his freak” from those who wouldn’t get it, and then shows her “freak”. Which happens to be Gilbert and Sullivan. Fascinating. And then they’re pulled out of the Turbolift. Why IS the Turbolift like this in Discovery, anyway? That makes no god damn sense.

Yeah, this was cute. 7.5. It’s kind of like proto SNW.

The Trouble With Edward - ST 4/10

Fuckin’ Edward. Always with this shit.

No, I dunno who Edward is.

More Enterprise. Pike is saying goodbye to a scientist under his watch who’s being promoted to Captain.

Her name is Captain Lucero, and she’s now the captain of a research vessel on the edge of Klingon space. Under her watch is a man named Edward. Edward is breeding Tribbles with genetic modifications to make them breed faster so they can be a good food source. Lucero is like “what in the fuck? No, that’s stupid, do not do that”. It doesn’t help that he’s wildly unethical.

Edward is weird, unlikable, and immoral. Not exactly the most popular dude on the starship. He’s also jealous of Lucero, who is likable, immediately connects with the crew, and of course, shuts down his dumb-ass plan about Tribbles.

This kind of guy 100% exists. Every single person has worked with this guy. We all have an Edward. BTW apparently he did this by putting his DNA into the Tribbles. What the hell?

He, of course, continues to do his dumb Tribble experiments, and the Tribbles begin to overrun the ship. This episode makes me wonder how the fuck Trouble with Tribbles happened. These Tribbles blew up a fucking research vessel, how did they EVER get onto the Enterprise??? They acted like they were unknown cute little pets there, this should be national news.

If Lucero makes any mistakes this episode, it’s not putting Edward in the brig. It’s a joke episode full of nonsense, so it’s not supposed to be serious. But this guy is LITERALLY crushed to death under a tidal wave of Tribbles while going “I’M NOT AN IDIOT, YOU’RE THE IDIOT”. Eventually she’s brought in front of a tribunal and defends herself by going “He was an idiot”

Everyone DOES have an Edward but this episode felt weirdly mean spirited

Ask Not - ST 2/10

A ship is under attack, and a young cadet, Cadet Sidhu, is in an exploding room. Some officers bring in a prisoner, who turns out to be Captain Pike who’s immediately like “okay, let me out.” Sidhu, of course, does not do that, he’s a prisoner. He tries to be manipulative about this. LEt me out or people will die. Your family will die. It will be your fault.

Eventually, after a lot of disgusting emotional manipulation, Pike goes “damn, she’s good. Okay, simulation over” and reveals it was all a test. I… that’s really shitty. This shouldn’t be allowed. This is some CIA shit.

Anyways she gets a job on board the Enterprise cause of this. I’ll just consider this noncanon.

Ephraim And Dot - ST 7/10

This is the first animated Star Trek thing since TAS. Wowee zowee.

It’s about a space Tardigrade named Ephraim. She is swept up onto the Enterprise as it floats by, during Space Seed of all episodes, and a little drone called Dot goes out to stop her from running amuck. What proceeds is some extreme slapstick of Ephraim going through the Enterprise and getting thrown around a lot. She lays some eggs, tries to protect those eggs.

Wow, I had no idea Naked Time took place at the same time as Space Seed. Oh shit, AND Tholian Web? Yeah, okay, it’s a big joke episode, it’s fine. It’s cute references. We even get the classic TOS fight music!

In the end, Dot saves Ephraim’s eggs, and then flies back off into space. This isn’t anything super great, but it’s cute.

The Girl Who Made The Stars - ENT 5.5/10

Another animated one! Cool. I don’t like the animation very much, though. It feels like a bootleg Christian kids movie. A young Michael Burnham wakes up from a nightmare, and his dad has to tell her a story to get her to fall asleep again. He tells some folk story about “the girl who made the stars”. You know, that classic kind of story where like, a young girl (shown as young Michael here) discovers some weird alien creature, and throws wood ash into the air to create the milky way. It’s very kidsy.

The story of the light inside this girl leading her to be a brave queen cures Michael of her fear of the dark, and she doesn’t need the night light anymore. Alright.

Cute enough, but the animation was REALLY distracting me.

Children of Mars - ENT 1/10

The sequel to Mars Needs Moms?

Two girls on Mars are going to school. Even in the future, school kids are dicks for no reason. Things in these two girls’ lives aren’t perfect, cause even in a perfect utopian future, this one girl’s miner dad on Mars can’t make time to go back and see her. Dog, a flight from the Earth to Mars takes like, 5 minutes. There’s prolly busses.

This shit has a really weirdly dour tone. There’s a weird sad song, the anxious unpleasant vibe of a middle school full of shitty awful kids. I get to listen to this really weird music as I watch these two teenagers beat the shit out of each other in slow motion. It’s some pretentious bullshit, I hate this. These two girls are then taken in to be disciplined, wheeeen

We find out Mars has been attacked, and countless are dead. We get flashes of the girls talking to their parents on Mars, as news flashes on the screen about the Mars attacks. 3000 dead. 3000?! THEY NUKED THE ENTIRE SURFACE OF THE PLANET. UTOPIA PLANITIA IS THE BIGGEST SHIPDOCK IN THE FEDERATION. Anyway…. the two girls then hold their hands, and the episode ends.

A fucking take on 9/11. I want to vomit. The stupid fucking Mars attacks in Picard are bad enough already, but fucking whooooof.

There’s really not a single good thing about this.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Lower Decks Season 2
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know very little about this show except it was made by nerds like me with a very different sense of humor. God, please be good.

Strange Energies - ENT 4/10

Our season starts with Mariner busting out of a Cardassian torture center, while Mariner is expositing about how her life is exhausting. This SHOW is exhausting. I was hoping they’d slow down a little, but no, still going a mile a minute. This is a hologram btw, we aren’t like at war with the Cardassians, I hope.

Mariner and her mom are getting along a lot better, and it’s rubbing the rest of the crew the wrong way. Our B plot is Tendi and Rutherford, again. Rutherford lost his memory and he’s a bit different. Tendi is worried he has some kind of deadly condition that effects cyborgs.

Mariner does a power washing on some ancient monuments that unleashes an energy wave that hits Ransom. He becomes a superpowered god and goes mad with power. As annoying as I find this plot point, it’s classic Trek and we haven’t seen it in a while.

Ransom becomes a giant head and tries to eat the ship. They need to short out his godliness by making him more powerful. Meanwhile, Tendi chases Rutherford around the ship with guns and stuff. She eventually admits its a lie and just wanted to cut into his brain to make sure he likes her. He finds it cool. That’s weird as hell but okay, if they’re good for each other.

They ignore Mariner’s suggestion to overpower him, and try to talk him down instead. It doesn’t work, so Mariner kicks him in the dick which depowers him. Okay. Mariner and her mom decide “okay maybe working together wasn’t working, let’s go back to the status quo”. All’s back to the status quo… except Boimler still isn’t here. How’s he doing on that ship with Riker anyway?

Poorly! Why did they make Riker like this anyway, it’s kinda weirding me out.

Kayshon, His Eyes Open - ENT 6/10

This episode introduces a few new characters. We need a replacement for Boimler, that’s Jet Manhaver, he’s a tightass who butts heads with Mariner a lot. And then we have the new security staff, that’s Kayshon, a Tamarian. Remember them, from like, the best episode? Darmok indeed.

Meanwhile, Boimler is still having trouble settling in on the Titan. Neli is complaining about how the Titan is a science recon ship but all they show it in is combat missions. That’s very Neli and while I don’t care, I do care, y’know?

Riker’s crew is like “man, the Enterprise D was so lame and boring”. It’s like it was made directly to annoy my chat lol

The Lower Deckers are going to a guy’s collection ship to reposses his stuff after he died. This sets off his security, and Kayshon is turned into a doll. Poor guy. Mariner and Jet are arguing a lot and fighting the entire time.

Riker’s team are infiltrating some Pakled. One of them is like “We got a ‘led over there”. DON’T SAY IT LIKE THAT. WE DON’T NEED TO INVENT SLURS. Riker’s crew is like “THIS IS WHAT STARFLEET IS ALL ABOUT, FIGHTING TO THE DEATH IN PHASER FIGHTS” and Boimler finally is like “What are you talking about? Starfleet is about exploring and boldly goingly, that’s what Starfleet is to me.” The others go “Oh yeah, actually, I got into Starfleet to study moss.” This is NOT how I expected this to go. That’s not the vibe of Lower Decks.

They eventually realize that NEITHER Mariner OR Jet should be in charge, and Tendi and Rutherford fix the problems very quickly.

Boimler gets cloned like Riker’s clone did, and one of them gets sent back to the Cerritos. They IMMEDIATELY replace Jet with him, and all’s well. Oh and Kayshon goes back to normal.

We’ll Always Have Tom Paris - ENT 3/10

Shaxs is back from the dead. Rutherford is freaked out, everyone is like “nah, bridge crew always come back from the dead it’s fine”. I’d say that’s funny but they kinda dwell on it for a while.

Meanwhile, there’s a few other plots. 1) Tom Paris is on the ship, and Boimler is trying to get him to sign a decorative plate. 2) Security is beefed up and causing problems 3) Tendi and Mariner are going to collect anti-flea cream or something for the doctor.

Tendi and Mariner are like “man we’re besties but we never hang out, why is that?” they draw attention to the fact that they never actually hang out much, which I kept complaining about in season 1. At least they realized it tbh? Anyways the thing they’re getting is actually a “caitian sex post”, which they of course break.

They keep trying to do things Mariner’s way, and it makes the problem even worse. They bet on billiards with some Nausicaans, and they of course get angry and break shit. There’s also a lot of stuff about “oh yeah, Tendi’s an Orion”. They didn’t talk about that too much in season 1, but yes, Orions have been commonly shown as evil pirates. She has a complex about this, but if SHE’S doing it, it’ll be fine.

We find out that Mariner is pansexual. Cool. She even namedrops “bad nonbinaries”. Sick.

This episode is all over hte place. A, B, AND C plots in a 20 minute episode is way, way too much.

Boimler goes nuts from a gasleak and Tom Paris tackles him.

Mariner and Tendi crash the shuttle into the ship. It bounces off the shields. THAT’S NOT HOW SHIELDS WORK. They only work on energy weapons. Okay.

The conclusion to the Rutherford plot is that he learns horrifying secrets of death and it makes him upset.

This epsiode was ALL over the place.

Mugato, Gumato - ENT 7/10

We start our episode with Mariner beating the shit out of Rutherford and Boimler. Boimler ends up throwing up. This is VERY Rick and Morty. They then go chat with the bartender, and he gives them a conspiracy theory that Mariner is part of Black Ops. Now they’re both anxious about her. They all need to go to a planet to find a Mugato. They’re from a TOS episode.

Meanwhile, we got another B plot. Tendi needs to scan some people who are avoiding it so she can impress the doctor. She is bad at asserting herself.

They find a Ferengi poaching operation, and shit goes south. They then find Mariner stabbing and eating Shaxs. Uh, okay. They run off into the wilderness, trying to hide.

We get a bunch of moments of them trying to survive in the wilderness. They barely do. They eventually come across Mariner, the only person not caught by the Ferengi, and she’s like “wtf no, I was sucking venom out, dog.” Yeah, she’s not a spy. She set that rumor herself.

Also turns out that the last holdout for people avoiding seeing the doctor… is THE DOCTOR HERSELF! She chases her around the ship, it’s fun. She breaks her arm, whoops.

Rutherford and Boimler use THEIR skills. They convince the Ferengi making a mugato perserve is way better for their profit margin. Hey man, when you don’t solve the problem with VIOLENCE, it’s good!! And also the jokes were pretty funny here. I like how it ends with them playing a board game they both love, and though MAriner barely understands it, she’s kicking their teeth in.

An Embarassment of Dooplers - ENT 5/10

The bridge crew has to take care of “Ambassador Doopler”, a guy who can duplicate if freaked out. It’s stressful as hell. But when they’re done, they get to go to the WILD party that’s being thrown every few years. Meanwhile, Boimler and Mariner are going to go to the party, cause Boimler’s clone was invited but is too busy.

Things go south on the station VERY quickly, they are NOT supposed to be there, cause the ship is under Doopler Quarantine. Why are the ship security dressed like Judge Dread?

Boimler and Mariner have a falling out cause they’re kinda both butthurt about things the other did to each other but never brought up until stress is happening. Boimler gets into the party in the end, but Mariner doesn’t. But even though the party is sick as hell, Boimler knows no one and wants to be with Mariner in the end, so he hangs with her in a shitty old bar.

Are we re-learning the same moral lesson every episode??

They fix the Doopler problem by making him angry. After they don’t get into the party after all, the captain just teleports him into th e party. I THINK that’d get you like, fired, but aight. Anyways they all have fun just partying on their own with their friends.

An Embarassment of Dooplers - ENT 6/10

The captain goes to the Pakled’s home planet, notably called “Pakled Planet”, and a Pakled prisoner escapes to their ship and requests asylum. They keep the captain on the planet until they give the prisoner back. Meanwhile, the B plot is Boimler is drafted into a group calling themselves The Redshirts. One of them is a Kzinti, which is sick as hell, I love the Kzinti.

The Pakled, Rumdar, is actually VERY obviously a spy. But he’s also incompetent because the Pakled’s one joke is they are very dumb. Of ALL the aliens to expand upon, why oh WHY did they decide to choose the Pakleds

The Kzinti does this thing from TAS. Yay. But actually this new friend group is kinda shitty. Not yay. They’re clearly not caring about Boimler as a person or his own person, and he needs to learn that his TRUE friends are his… friends. Yeah it’s not the deepest plot.

Rutherford gets blown up like a balloon by a science experiment, and Tendi gets eaten. This episode is FILLED with kinks.

Tendi LITERALLY TFS INTO A BUG. Were the writers actually trying to fit in as many crews as they could? Anyways Boimler fix this by being not GLAMAROUS, but by being Starfleet, and is abandoned by his new “friends”.

Meanwhile they find the spy floating in space. He spaced himself cause ha ha Pakleds are stupid. He doesn’t die because “Pakleds are strong”. They counter spy him easily cause he’s dumb and then they fly off, all’s fine.

It ends with them prank calling Armus. Fuck that guy, fun joke.

Where Pleasant Fountains Lie - ENT 6.5/10

The crew saves a civilization from being enslaved by an evil computer voiced by THE MAN, JEFFREY COMBS. He’s Agimus the Evil Computer. I love it. Meanwhile, Commander Billups, the chief engineer, turns out to be PRINCE OF HYSPERIA, fantasy kingdom. But he gave up his royalty to be an engineer. But if he ever HAS SEX he’ll lose his engineering powers and have to go back to being a prince. What the fuck? Alright, fair enough. “Sorry mom, but there’s only one lady I love, and she’s 2 stories tall and pumped full of dilithium.” You love women?! Rutherford and Billups go over to their ship to fix their “Dragonbreath engines”. Apparently they renamed everything to sound magical.

Oh thank GOD. Boimler and Mariner crashland on a ship due to an anomaly, and now they’re stuck with Evil Jeffrey Combs computer. Yay, more Jeffrey Combs!!! It drives a wedge between the two of them, cause of course it does, but also that’d been happening for a while, cause of the whole Titan problem that I SWEAR we’ve solved THREE TIMES NOW.

Meanwhile, Billups fixes his mom’s ship and heads back, all’s good. Whoops, the ship’s engine explodes, killing her and Rutherford. I mean, we know what’s going on, obviously. Billups immediately resigns, cause he blames himself. Very obvious plot here, but that’s fine. Tendy immediately finds out that it’s fake, and Rutherford saves Billups from sex.

Turns out Boimler was PRETENDING to be angry at Mariner so he could use Combs to power a distress beacon. It’s pretty funny, honestly, in the end. Agimus is put in a wall with a hundred other evil computers, the end.

I, Excretus - ENT 4/10

Due to captain incompetence, the lower decks almost die. They are not happy. I still don’t find this fun.

A Starfleet drill instructor is here to do some kind of drills to test the ship. I am confused by the idea of Starfleet having drill instructors, but she’s Pandronian, from TAS. Cool, I like all the TAS references. Her name is Shari yn Yem, and she gamifies running the ship, for some reason. They gotta do holodeck stuff, the lower decks are assigned higher bridge duties, while the bridge officers are assigned lower deck stuff.

We get lots of weird scenes iwth them doing strange stuff like uh… Tendi has to kill a Klingon who DESPERATELY wants to die? Mariner has to be in an old wild west town. It’s a bunch of rapid fire references. Boimler gets a Borg one, while everyone else fails their’s, he succeeds easily… but wants to be perfect, so he does it over and over.

Mariner also has to go in one where it’s The Naked Now, and she runs into the simulated ship having a sex orgy. This is DEFINITELY some kind of HR violation. Does Starfleet have HR?

Things aren’t going much better to the other side either, btw.

After failing some more, they both realize that grass isn’t greener on the other side. But yeah, the drills are actually rigged so that the drill instructor can prove her job is actually worth having. Uh, okay. But she can’t be done and send in their failing numbers until Boimler is done. And he’s still not done.

They… for some reason send the ship into a fucking crystalline entity in order to freak her out, basically holding her hostage and doing scary shit that they do all the time. It all works out, and all it took was getting Boimler traumatized. But the bridge crew promise to treat the lower decks better, AND they make thgeir food replicator y’know, normal. Before it was like, segregated cause ha ha joke? Okay if we can do away with those jokes, I’ll be very happy.

wej Duj - ENT 8/10

We get some little vignets about Vulcan and Klingon lower decks. They are… basically what you’d expect someone would write about when asked to do alien lower decks. A Klingon tries to impress his new captain after that captain killed the old one, so he can be first officer. Meanwhile, a Vulcan feels like something is off, and goes to tell the captain. Everyone thinks she’s a freak for following “feelings”.

Meanwhile, back on the Cerritos, everyone is hanging out with the bridge crew today… except Boimler. He doesn’t really have an “in” like the others. So he tries to get a bridge crew buddy. It goes poorly.

Boimler lies about being from Hawaii to get an in with Ransom. Oh great, time for the liar revealed trope. Rutherford is like “Dude, what’s more important? Having a commander friend, or being true to yourself?” And the answer is obvious. Commander friend.

Okay, this Vulcan, T’Lyn, I kinda like her? All the Vulcans around her are snooty assholes and she’s like “I just want to work.” “The others can see how unstable you are, T’Lyn.” “Well if they’ve been watching me so closely they must be aware I do not care about their opinions”. I like her :D

The Klingon and Federation plotlines collide. The Klingon captain was supplying bombs to the Pakleds. His new first in command is like “wow this is REALLY not honorable?” and then the Cerritos shows up and is attacked by the Klingons and Pakleds. The Vulcans then show up to save the day and we get a big fight scene.

The Klingon takes command and then orders a retreat, as it’s nto their battle. The Pakleds are easily defeated, with T’Lyn’s new project having made the Vulcan ship even stronger, and it works out.

The episode ends with Boimler being a upper rank buddy for a new cadet on the ship. It’s kinda cute.

…Actually it REALLY ends showing “Borg Lower Decks”, with them just sitting there regenerating. Fair. Legitimately good episode!!

First First Contact - ENT 7.5/10

Captain Freeman is working with Captain Sonya Gomez with a first contact mission. She ALSO learns she’s going to be promoted. Remember Gomez? She’s the ensign who spilled coffee on Picard. We loved her but she was only in a few episodes. It’s nice to see her.

Mariner tells the senior staff that the captain is transfering off. They are VERY angry about this, especially when they find out none of them are going with her. It IS kinda rude tbh. Meanwhile, Tendi sees the doctor delete her from the system, and thinks she’s gonna be transferred off the ship.

Shit suddenly goes bad, Gomez’s ship is suddenly knocked out by a planetoid hitting itt and shut down. It’ll crash into the planet and do catastrophic damage. The Cerritos comes up with a very dangerous and risky mission to try and rescue them. It’ll be dangerous, but if they work together, it can work.

Holy shit, Cetacean Ops!!!! I’VE ALWAYS WANTED TO SEE CETACEAN OPS! Boimler has to go into a flooded place to do a thing, and ends up drowning, while Mariner is thrown from the ship and flies towards the anomaly…. but is saved by that Andorian who was in this season a few times who’s been a real bitch to her! Huh, okay.

They save the day, and all is well. First contact goes well, no one dies. The Andorian’s name is Jennifer, she’s cute. The captain also turns down the promotion… but uh, finds out she’s ACTUALLY under arrest for a suspected bombing that blew up Pakled Planet. Bullshit of course, but y’know. We end on a sad note as everyone sees the captain taken away in cuffs. To be continued.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 225 - Magical Bocco 20541
entry.content:
More random MSX games. One thing you need to know about the MSX is that it has a LARGE homebrew scene. People are making games for this thing cosntantly, of all shapes and sizes. A lot of them have the energy of a first project, someone just kinda testing the waters to practice coding. Some of them are really in depth projects, almost indistinguishable from retro games, except for maybe some more modern quality of life.

And then some are shitposts.

Magical Bocco 20541 is a shitpost. It was made by meichnpp in late 2024, so it’s pretty recent. You play as Bocco and her best friend, cat, and go on an adventure to do… something. Honestly you’re mostly wandering around different areas and exploring. The energy is absurdist. The art is incredible. The gameplay is simple but effective. It’s a two-party member RPG.

NPCs are standing around and kind of give SOME lore and vibes, but most of the time you’ll walk up to them and they say “hello!” or something that’s not important, while cat wanders the screen randomly. Basically you want to travel around, pick up chests, and move forward. There’s very simple puzzles.

The main thing setting this game apart from others is its absurdist art style. It’s very MSPaint, and is really charming. There’s some AMAZING enemies you can fight, but I don’t want to spoil them. This game isn’t long, and there’s not a LOT to it, but it’s a cute little thing for a bit of a distraction.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Discovery Season 2 episodes 11 - 15
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know literally nothing about Discovery, except for The Burn, and that it leads into Strange New Worlds. So uh… I’ve been dreading this one. But hey, who knows! Maybe it’ll surprise me!!

The Red Angel - DIS 2/10

We start with a eulogy for Airiam. They keep talking about how important she was to them. I wish they had… shown that throughout the show. Most of us in chat couldn’t even remember her name today. It ALSO doesn’t help that I know the reason they killed Airiam off is cause her prosthetics were too much to put on every day.

This scene goes on forever. We knew literally nothing about her until she episode she died in.

Also, think about when Dr. Culber died. We didn’t even see anyone’s reaction. No one gave a shit except his boyfriend, who found out way later. No one gave a shit at all, they didn’t even look at the recording to see who killed him.

HOW COULD THE TORMENT NEXUS BEGIN CREATING TORMENT?

How does this woman still have a job? How did she get it to begin with? She’s awful. Spock blames time travel btw. Sure. Anyways Tilly comes in and goes “I found data of the biometrics of the Red Angel. It’s you, Michael”. Hey, I called it! Also this twist is very half-assed.

The only survivors of Section 31, Georgiou’s group, shows up. Why the fuck are they still allowed to exist? Shut them down, their AI they built is evil and trying to kill everyone. Also they talk about what Project Daedalus is, it’s a time travel thing. They even reference the Temporal Cold War. God, no. Why the fuck would you bring that back up???

Georgiou makes the moves on Stamets, who is extremely and obviously uncomfortable. “You do know he’s gay, right?” CAN WE SHOVE THIS WOMAN OUT AN AIRLOCK. Also I guess it’s referencing the rampant pansexuality of the mirror universe, cause apparently Mirror Stamets was pan? They even name pansexuality directly, which is wild to me, most people pretend that it doesn’t even exist.

The section 31 guy tells Michael that he got her parents killed, by assigning them to a job to make the time travel suit. He and his team fucked up and they got killed. She beats the shit out of him.

Okay I just realized something about Culber’s perdicament. People are like “You’re a new person, brand new”. So basically, he was torn apart then rebuilt from new molecules beamed in from somewhere else.

That’s called transporting. He got transported.

“Captain, we need to possibly sacrifice my life to capture the Red Angel, it’s that or all sentient life dies”. WHY DO WE NEED TO CAPTURE HER THOUGH? She’s been helping out everywhere. Honestly, I think we should leave her to fix the god damn timeline. Start by killing Georgiou. Then we won’t have any stupid future Section 31 movie.

Michael and Ash start making out again. Why are we here? Just to suffer?

They make a huge deal about some random new character coming onto the bridge. She’s Airam’s replacement. Played by the same actress, actually.

Here’s a fun scene of Michael screaming and gasping as she dies. Just play the Star Trek theme over this for maximum incongruence.

Eventually, we succeed, the Red Angel shows up, we capture her. And they unmask her to reveal it’s… NOT Michael! IT’S HER MOM! With her… biopattern and brain pattern? I dunno, whatever.

Perpetual Infinity - DIS 2/10

We get a flashback showing Michael’s childhood, and the death of her parents. But obviously not. She wakes up, having been fully resucitated. She’s told her mother is unconscious on the planet, but alive.

The section 31 guy I could never remember the name of is captured by the evil AI and injected with some kind of things, with a very graphic and violent scream. It’s gross. RIP that guy I guess. His corpse is taken over and controlled by the AI. Why does Section 31 have borg-esque nanotech??? It’s actually BETTER, cause no one realizes it, it’s subterfuge.

Michael watches the mission logs from her mother’s suit. She sees that she tries to get back over and over but fails. And also she’s in a post apocalypse. Eventually she switches to trying to fix this problem instead. Michael is kinda pissy she’s focusing on trying to fix this issue and not see her. Come on, Michael. Dr. Burnham is like “Just delete the sphere archive, that’ll solve ALL of this” “No, we’re protecting it!” “Then we’re done here.” She’s become a distant Time Protector type character.

They actually DO try to delete it, but the data itself refuses to be deleted.

The section 31 guy’s corpse is a lot more malevolent than usual. But everyone’s just ignoring it. If anything I’d say this is extremely accurate to how people in power misuse that power and everyone who has the ability to stop them just doesn’t. He teams up with Georgiou and asks her to help kill Dr. Burnham AND steal the sphere data. Please don’t fall for this shit, Georgiou.

They come up with a plan, though. They’re gonna upload the sphere data into the suit, and send it into the future so it survives, AND is away from the evil AI. They’ll also use some dark matter shit to keep her here.

Georgiou goes up to Dr. Burnham and all the guards just let her, and as she’s clearly menacing, they just stand there and watch. Why are they even here? After realizing that hey, maybe what she’s doing is OBVIOUSLY dumb, she stops the transfer and sends Ash over to stop Leland. Ash is stabbed. Whoops. Maybe you should have DESTROYED the thing downloading the data???

Leland goes down guns blazing to get the data, deactivating the suit. They release the containment field keeping Dr. Burnham there, and she returns to the future, without the suit this time, stuck in the future. But hey, the AI doesn’t have all the data I guess.

Things are distraught, but for some reason this clears the air between Michael and Spock so they’re on speaking terms again?? Also Ash escaped in an escape pod.

The Valley of the Shadow of Death - DIS 4/10

I take a look at my life and realize there’s nothing left.

They’re heading to a place called Boreth, some Klingon monastary. They have some time crystals, and Pike is going to try and negotiate with them for one. L’Rell helps him get an audience. Meanwhile, Michael follows a lead for a possibly rogue Section 31 ship to try and get back at them.

This Klingon planet is fucking Moria. Pike meets the guardians of time, or whatever. They’re ominous and mysterious, but then he ends his whole thing, about how maybe Pike can prove himself worthy before the time crystals, but “only time will tell”. I wish it then ended with him laughing and going “I always wanted to say that”. Also apparently this guy is the son of Voq? “Time flows differently here”. HOW DOES THIS KEEP ITSELF STABLE

Michael and Spock go and find that the Section 31 ship spaced everyone. They have someone in it called Gant, who was on the Shenzhou, so Michael knows him. He’s pretty clearly controlled by Control, I dunno why they even bother rescuing him, they KNOW Control can do this.

Oh no, PIKE IS GOING TO BECOME DAVROS

Yeah I already knew about this cause it’s important in Strange New Worlds. But this is a pretty good moment. Pike is shown he will, in the future, suffer a VERY bad fate where he’ll become crippled and barely alive. The Klingon there is like “you can still walk away, and stop that future from occuring”, but that’d involve risking hte lives of everyone else. So Pike sacrifices himself to save others, you know, a common theme of Starfleet. Cause Pike is good.

Holy SHIT, Jett Reno is back. God thank you. Discovery’s quality is directly proportional to its amount of Jett Reno. There’s actually a lot of good character moments in this? Not with Michael’s portion of the plot, but with Jett, the B characters on the bridge, and of course Pike. Also Jett Reno is gay! I mean yeah, look at her.

Wow Gant was roboticized what a surprise. Eggman’s plan is coming to fruition. Whatever.

They shoot a hole in gant and nanogoo floods out of him and tries to move into Michael. What the fuck is this shit? This is dumber than whatever they did with the Borg in First Contact.

Everyone gets back to Discovery, to find that Control is sending all 30-ish Section 31 ships to it. They get 30 ships? You remember Wolf 359? That was all the ships that they could get to fight the Borg. That was 40 ships. And that’s like 150 years from now.

WHY IS THE ENTIRE FLEET SECTION 31

Anyways they announce abandon ship and prepare to blow up Discovery. This episode was better than the other recent ones, cause the stuff that’s NOT the main plot is pretty good, and it’s MOSTLY been main plot recently.

*Such Sweet Sorrow Part 1 - DIS 3.5/10

Discovery is preparing to abandon ship and self destuct. Enterprise comes to help. They make some weird bridges go between the two ships. Those things look DESIGNED to be ripped apart dramatically in a battle, some people are TOTALLY gonna die on those bridges.

They give some reasons why they’re getting rid of hologram communications. I feel like they keep reinventing hologram communication every 50 years going “why don’t we do this?!” then rediscover it’s a bad idea, cause they’ll do this again in DS9.
