They try to blow it up, but the sphere data has combined with Discovery now and refuses to be blown up. Also, Michael touched the time crystal and is now getting visions. She sees all of Enterprise being boarded and blown up by Section 31, and everyone being killed.

Things are bad, but how can they fix this? Michael has a plan. Send Discovery into the future, so that the data isn’t destroyed, but so Control can’t find it. I dunno why we don’t just stop Control but whatever. They decide to send Michael. She IS the special boy. But also they determine that her doing this will result in her setting hte signals. Uh, okay, sure, whatever. I guess I still called it.

Stamets and Culber fix things up and part on better terms after Culber got a talking to from Jett. He was fixed by the gentle touch of a lesbian.

Remember Po? She was in one of the Short Treks. Tilly calls her in to ask for her help creating enough power to power their makeshift red-angel suit. Kinda wild that that short trek is so important.

What the fuck, Sarek and his wife show up? Sarek is like “Our katras are connected so I knew you’d be here”. YEAH, BUT HOW DID THEY GET HERE SO FAST? ENTERPRISE IS 50 MINUTES BEHIND THEM, CONTROL 10 MINUTES BEHIND THAT.

People decide to stay with Michael and go into the future with her. Possibly forever. Not everyone. Ash is staying behind, because “if there’s anyone who can stop something like Control from happening again, it’s someone in the gray areas”. DOG, THAT’S HOW THIS HAPPENED IN THE FIRST PLACE. We then get a lot of scenes of characters making last logs to their friends and loved ones. And then we get one last monologue from Pike to everyone on the ship before he heads back to Enterprise. Everyone stands to honor him as he leaves the bridge for the last time. Thank you Pike, for making this season tolerable.

Why in the fuck does Control have so many ships. Is this Star Wars??? Anyways prepare for battle.

Such Sweet Sorrow Part 2 - DIS 2.5/10

We get a very uninteresting battle. I love how they shout “EVASIVE MANEUVERS” then the ships stay completely still. At least we get little scenes of the Engineering crew creating the suit.

I love when the shuttles fly past and it does the doppler effect. This is space y’all.

“RENO, GO DO THIS THING” “I’m going, I’m going, get off my ass! Uh, sir.” She says to the captain. I love Reno. This is apparently a moment that’s been claimed as a sign of Kurtzman’s bad writing. This scene is prolly improv, y’all. She’s a comic.

Stamets is gravely injured. Are we burying our gays again?

Also, Po comes back and gives them advice on how to blow up the drones. This is moving VERY fast and is hard to focus on.

Then suddenly we get a slow spin around with Spock and Michael looking at the suit. “It’s ready. It’s time.” and then they admire their work. Uh, not the fucking time toots, IT’S TIME TO QUANTUM LEAP.

THIS SCENE LASTS SO FUCKING LONG, Y’ALL REALLY GOTTA GET GOING.

The Section 31 guy, still a robot, beams over to Discovery, enters the bridge, shoots at them a bunch, then leaves. They’re like “HE’S GOING FOR THE SPHERE DATA!” Then why the fuck did he even bother entering the bridge?!

A torpedo lodges itself in the Enterprise… which Michael had seen in a vision. She didn’t tell anyone? Dude.

Things are looking bad, but then the Klingons show up to help, alongside some Kelpians with some Ba’ul ships. I guess they stole them and did a fucking Battlefield Earth by teaching themselves how to pilot them.

Awww, Culber shows up to say Stamets. He announces that he fucked up, and was being very stupid (he was, he just teleported, dude). First time I felt a pang of emotion in this episode, which is trying to be full of them. FTR, Stamets is THE takeaway from these seasons so far. He’s by far the best part. I apologize for saying I hated him in the first two episodes.

Michael goes through time.

My god, it’s full of stars.

Holy shit there’s some really bad flashing, jesus. Anyways she goes back in time to do all the stuff she already did. Saving herself, setting up the different signals. I like how it reshows scenes of the characters going “these signals are here for a reason!” but there… isn’t one. It’s a time paradox loop. She’s just doing these things because they’ve already been done. No one actually came up with the plan to do these things.

There’s a fight scene with Robo Leland and Georgiou and the chief of security I forget the name of. It’s the best fight scene, because they had to keep the camera still due to gravity effect shenanigans, and we could actually see the fight.

To get rid of the photon torpedo, the Admiral sacrifices herself by using a manual lever. Oh no, not… her, the worst admiral I can think of off the top of my head. Okay MAYBE she’s a little better than like, the TOS guy who hated the windsock, or the TNG land of confusion guy who did the iran contra affair.

Michael comes back, and has to say goodbye to Spock. He’s like “Michael, you are my balance, you made me who I am, I do not know how I will survive without you.” Yes. Michael, the OC, is the reason Spock is Spock. :) She’s the reason we like him.

Discovery blasts off, following Michael, into the future.

Wait, ALL of Control put itself into Leland? Georgiou kills Leland, and destroys Control completely. Then… why are we going into the future??? They were going there to escape Control, but Control was on the ship anyway, so that wouldn’t have worked. Also, why would they put all of the hivemind control into one singular being, one dude MADE OF MEAT??

This is the dumbest thing of all time.

Then we get a “where are they now” for everyone back in the past. They’re all being questioned by Starfleet suits who believe they’re lying about something.

THEY MADE ASH TYLER THE COMMANDER OF SECTION 31?! THE DUDE WORKED THERE FOR TWO WEEKS. I mean I guess he’s LITERALLY the only person in Section 31 left but DUDE.

Anyways they’re all sworn to secrecy. I guess thats a good way to make sure none of this nonsense corrupts the rest of the timeline.

Many months later, they find the seventh signal 51,000 light years away signalling they made it to the future safely. Spock also shaves his beard. They move on with Enterprise-y things. “Let us see what the future holds”.

This was… better than season 1, at least.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 224 - Transball
entry.content:
It should not surprise you to learn I immediately jumped on playing this game.

Lunar lander style games are way way WAY out of style, but they were really big on PCs in the late 80s. It’s a pretty simple concept. You aim your ship and fire to thrust in that direction, and try to fly around objects and terrain while not letting gravity slam you into everything it can. Most of those games involve you landing softly on something, though this is a bit different.

Transball is a game by Santiago Ontañón Villar, some MSX homebrew from 2016. Your goal is to collect the transball and then escape the top of the screen. Sometimes this is easy. A lot of the times it very much is not. Your ship controls like most lunar lander sim games, unless you have the ball, where it KINDA feels like Knuckles Chaotix, and can influence your movement, and you can influence it. The best strategy I found is very slow pulses to move you and the ball forward.

There are only 16 stages, but it’ll still take you an hour to two hours to beat this. It’s challenging but rewarding to master. There’s really not a lot to it, it IS MSX homebrew, but it’s expertly made and worth a glance if you’re messing with the MSX.
